requirements.content: Produce a complete trip plan covering 3 days in Pittsburgh.
Day 1 itinerary must focus on the University of Pittsburgh/Oakland area and include a visit to the Cathedral of Learning.
Provide recommendations for famous Pittsburgh food/meal spots for the trip.
Recommended restaurants must be places locals go to (not expensive or fancy).
Day 1 itinerary must include a coffee stop after lunch at La Prima Espresso Company (3400 Fifth Ave).
Day 1 dinner plan must be updated to The Porch at Schenley.
Provide a description of the Andy Warhol Museum.
Day 2 itinerary must include visiting the Strip District in the morning.
Day 2 itinerary must include having lunch at Wholey's.
Day 2 itinerary must include visiting the Andy Warhol Museum after lunch.
After lunch on Day 2, the plan must go to downtown (instead of the Andy Warhol Museum).
Downtown time on Day 2 must prioritize outdoor places/activities rather than indoor options.
Recommend Gaucho Parrilla Argentina menu items that comply with the constraint of not eating strongly gamey meat.
Day 3 plan must be for visiting and exploring CMU.
Day 3 plan must include both CMU's main campus and the buildings on the Craig St side.
Day 3 dinner must be on Craig St.
Day 3 plan must be updated so lunch includes bread and there is a café coffee stop after lunch.
Day 3 plan must include a single place where lunch and coffee are had together (at the same time).

outputs.content: Complete 3-day Pittsburgh trip plan (overall structure and completion/closure)
Day 1 itinerary near University of Pittsburgh/Oakland (Cathedral of Learning area) with coffee and meal plan updates
Day 2 itinerary (Strip District + downtown + Mount Washington), revised to prioritize outdoor downtown spots and user preferences
Recommendations for famous/local Pittsburgh food and local-favorite restaurants (not fancy/expensive), especially for meals during the trip
Description of the Andy Warhol Museum (what it is like and what to do there)
Day 3 itinerary focused on exploring CMU (main campus + Craig St side) with lunch/coffee preferences and dinner on Craig St (revised to combine lunch+coffee at one place)
Menu item recommendations for Gaucho Parrilla Argentina that fit the user's 'no strongly gamey meat' dietary constraint

dialogue_summary: Plan a 3-day trip to Pittsburgh with day-by-day itineraries centered on specific neighborhoods/campuses, including local-food recommendations and adjustments for coffee, dining preferences, and dietary constraints.

actions.action_text: Assistant provides the full updated Day 1 itinerary.
actions.evidence_quote:
### **Pittsburgh Day 1: Oakland local tour (final)**

- **09:00 - Breakfast: [Pamela's Diner](https://pamelasdiner.com/)** (pancakes, Lyonnaise potatoes)
- **10:30 - Morning: [Cathedral of Learning](http://www.tour.pitt.edu/tour/cathedral-learning)** (Commons Room, Nationality Rooms)
- **12:30 - Lunch: [Primanti Bros.](https://primantibros.com/)** (Pittsburgh-style sandwich)
- **14:00 - Coffee: [La Prima Espresso Company](http://laprima.com/)** (3400 Fifth Ave)
- **15:30 - Afternoon: [Phipps Conservatory](https://www.phipps.conservatory.org/)** (conservatory and themed gardens)
- **18:30 - Dinner: [The Porch at Schenley](https://www.dineattheporch.com/)** (wood-fired pizza and local ingredients)
actions.speaker: assistant

outcomes.description: Description of the Andy Warhol Museum (what it is like and what to do there)

actions.action_text: Ask for a description of the Andy Warhol Museum
actions.evidence_quote: What is the Andy Warhol Museum like?
actions.speaker: user

actions.action_text: Describe the Andy Warhol Museum's focus, scale, and collections
actions.evidence_quote: **The Andy Warhol Museum** is dedicated to the life and work of Andy Warhol, the pop art icon from Pittsburgh. It’s the largest museum in North America devoted to a single artist. Seven floors cover everything from early commercial design to Campbell’s soup cans and Marilyn Monroe portraits, plus experimental film and archives.
actions.speaker: assistant

actions.action_text: Provide examples of interactive exhibits and activities
actions.evidence_quote: You can try silkscreen techniques and the “Silver Clouds” helium balloon room—it’s interactive, not just viewing.
actions.speaker: assistant

outcomes.description: Menu item recommendations for Gaucho Parrilla Argentina that fit the user's 'no strongly gamey meat' dietary constraint

actions.evidence_quote: Here are **Gaucho Parrilla Argentina** suggestions.
actions.speaker: assistant

actions.action_text: Assistant recommends Lomo (Filet Mignon) as lean and less gamey
actions.evidence_quote: Lomo (Filet Mignon): Lean and clean-tasting; less gamey than other cuts.
actions.speaker: assistant

actions.action_text: Assistant recommends Pollo a la Parrilla (Grilled Chicken) as a light option
actions.evidence_quote: Pollo a la Parrilla (Grilled Chicken): With lemon, thyme, and pickled red onion; light and fresh.
actions.speaker: assistant

actions.action_text: Assistant recommends Camarones (Grilled Shrimp) to avoid meat flavor
actions.evidence_quote: Camarones (Grilled Shrimp): Rosemary and olive oil; good when you want to avoid meat flavor entirely.
actions.speaker: assistant

actions.action_text: Assistant recommends Pescado (Fish) as a fresh, light option
actions.evidence_quote: Pescado (Fish): "Fish of the day" or fish sandwich (Pescado con Pan) with lemon and arugula—fresh and light.
actions.speaker: assistant

actions.action_text: Assistant recommends sides Maiz (grilled corn) and Vegetales (grilled vegetables)
actions.evidence_quote: Sides: Maiz (grilled corn) and Vegetales (grilled vegetables) are signature options.
actions.speaker: assistant

actions.action_text: Assistant suggests sauces (Chimichurri or Ajo) to balance stronger flavors
actions.evidence_quote: Sauces: Table Chimichurri or Ajo (garlic) sauce can balance stronger flavors.
actions.speaker: assistant

actions.action_text: Assistant warns to avoid Parrillada Mixta or Asado Plate which can include offal or strong sausage
actions.evidence_quote: Avoid: Parrillada Mixta or Asado Plate (mixed grill) can include offal or strong sausage—skip if you’re sensitive.
actions.speaker: assistant

actions.action_text: Assistant provides detailed itinerary items and suggestions for Day 3 (times, sites, lunch, Craig St, dinner).
actions.evidence_quote:
10:00 - CMU main campus – The Fence, Gates Hillman Complex

- **12:30 - Lunch: [Everyday Noodles](https://www.everydaynoodles.net/)** – Taiwanese dumplings and noodles in Squirrel Hill. (Try the shrimp dumplings.)

- **14:30 - Craig St area** – **Mellon Institute:** Classic columned building; a CMU icon and film location. **Software Engineering Institute (SEI):** Modern research buildings.

- **18:00 - Dinner: [Union Grill Oakland](https://www.uniongrilloakland.com/)** – A Craig St favorite.
